observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary journal
entries to correct the understatement of the Due to BIR account; and
b. Ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted on time to avoid
surcharges/penalties as may be imposed by the BIR pursuant to BIR RR
No. 11-2018.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to reclassify the unliquidated
cash advance of the former Municipal Mayor amounting to ₱1,174,103.30
to the Other Receivables account; and
The proposed adjusting journal entry is as follows:
Particulars Debit Credit
Other Receivables ₱1,174,103.30
Advances to Officers and Employees ₱1,174,103.30
b. Exhaust all legal and administrative remedies to recover the unliquidated
cash advances from the former Municipal Mayor.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Exert efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents to establish
the existence and validity for claiming the receivables;
b. Exert all efforts to collect the receivables accounts from the persons
responsible. If all efforts are futile, file the necessary request for write-off
of dormant receivables that are deemed uncollectible, supported with
completed documentation as listed in Annex 8, COA Circular No. 2023-
008, provided with index tabs, for easy reference;
c. Evaluate the credit risk of the receivables amounting to ₱2,570,480.30
and establish the corresponding loss allowance in accordance with
IPSAS 41, to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements; and
d. Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the necessary journal
entries to correct the understatement of the various Receivable accounts
and the other affected accounts. The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry
is as follows:

c. Exert efforts to locate documents relative to the unaccounted balance of

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
ii. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each Municipal Road and its

Network; and
iv. Ensure disclosure of the total Road Network System in the Notes

b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Network

Accountant to exert more effort to reconcile the reciprocal accounts and
cause the elimination thereof in the consolidated financial statements.

accordance with the specific guidelines set in the Revised Manual on the

Management require the
GFPS to:

a) Create/prepare the
GAD Agenda, copy
furnish the Audit Team;

b) Ensure that the GAD
Plan and Budget and GAD
Accomplishment Report
are in line with the GAD
Agenda, supported with
pertinent documents;

c) Make certain that
gender issues are
apparent supported by
evidence such as sex-
disaggregated data,
mandates are
GAD-related, and
Objectives and Gender
issue Projects/Programs/
Activities (P/P/A) are
aligned with the identified
gender issues. Non-GAD-
specific P/P/As are
budgeted under attributed
programs subject to the
use of HGDG tools to
determine that programs
and projects undertaken
are gender-responsive.

d) Ensure that any
deviation from the
identified gender issues,
activities, results and/or
the targets in the GPB, the
reasons for the deviations,
as well as the factors that
have facilitated or
hindered the implementation
of the DILG-endorsed
and/or LGU-approved
GPB are discussed or
disclosed in the
Variance/Remarks portion
of the Accomplishment
Report; and

e) Inform the Local Chief
Executive of any changes
during the adoption of the
DILG–approved GAD Plan
and Budget to the GAD
Accomplishment Report
for notification of the
DILG Regional Office.

We further recommended
that the management
judiciously prepare the
GAD Plan and Budget so
that the budgeted amounts
and PPAs are
implementable and
realistic.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any noted variances between the actual amounts in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amount (SCBAA) and Statement of Financial Performance (SFPe).
observations.reason: No disclosures were made in the Notes to Financial Statements for the discrepancies between the actual amounts in the SCBAA and SFPe.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort to locate documents pertaining to the reciprocal accounts, reconcile and cause the elimination thereof in the consolidated financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant would look into the source of the discrepancies in order to facilitate the reconciliation of these accounts.
observations.reason: The Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds remained unreconciled at yearend.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the GAD Focal Person to: a) Prioritize capacity development of its GFPS and/or similar GAD mechanism, as well as its employees on GAD concepts, principles, tools and processes like gender sensitivity, gender audit, gender analysis, gender-responsive planning and budgeting, gender-responsive project design and implementation and institutional strengthening, among others. b) Refer to the DILG-issued “Guide on the Review and Endorsement of LGU’s GAD Plan and Budget and Review of LGU’s GAD Accomplishment Report” in the preparation of succeeding GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Reports. c) Conduct a gender analysis on the GAD PPAs of succeeding years through the administration of the HGDG Tools to ensure that only the PPAs attributable to GAD are included in the GPB.
observations.managementAction: There were no attributed PPAs for CY 2023. The GAD PB and AR were prepared with five clear gender issues out of 24 statements. The GAD Focal Person and key personnel attended trainings on GAD. The DILG approved the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and the GAD Accomplishment Report (AR) was prepared in accordance thereof.
observations.reason: The correctness and alignment of 19 entries in the GAD PB and GAD AR cannot be checked with the LGUs’ GAD PPAs due to unclear gender issues and concerns of various LGUs; Data or information to support the gender issue not provided or cited; 27 Performance Targets and Indicators were general and not expressed in qualitative and quantitative measurements. There were no attributed programs in the GPB for CY 2023. HGDG tools were not utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management maintain subsidiary ledgers for GAD for each account and assign SL codes thereto, through the GAD focal person and the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate the determination of GAD-related expenses and other GAD-related financial transactions, and to monitor and properly account for the GAD funds, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2021-008.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledgers were maintained for GAD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to: i. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the net understatement of the Due to BIR account and the misstatements of other affected accounts; ii. Exert efforts to determine the cause of the over-remittance of ₱366,982.82 and prepare adjusting entries if warranted to establish the correct balance of the Due to BIR account; and iii. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each taxpayer to ensure correctness of remitted tax withheld. b) Ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted on time to avoid surcharges/penalties as may be imposed by the BIR pursuant to BIR RR No. 11-2018.
observations.managementAction: The MACCO made several adjustments in CY 2023 for errors incurred in prior years to establish the correct balance of the Due to BIR account. The MACCO is still in the process of establishing the correct balance of the Due to BIR account and will commence the reconciliation to determine the correct balance of the account for fair presentation in the financial statements.
observations.reason: The journal entries pertaining to the identified misstatements have not been taken up in the books of accounts. The MACCO is still in the process of establishing the correct balance of the Due to BIR account. No subsidiary ledgers for each taxpayer are being maintained. Taxes withheld for the month of May were remitted intact to the BIR in June. The amounts withheld for other months were not remitted intact to the BIR the following month.

observations.recommendation: Prepare a request for write-off of the receivable account following the requirements and procedures cited in COA Circular 2016-005 for the fair presentation of the assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: A request for write-off was submitted by management for the KKK loans amounting to ₱337,500.00 but was returned by the previous audit team to management due to incomplete supporting documents. No new request had been submitted at year-end.
observations.reason: No new request had been submitted at year-end due to incomplete supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary journal entries to correct the understatement of the PPE
accounts and overstatement of the Other Receivables Account.
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry is as follows:

Particulars Debit Credit
PPE 100,000.00
Other Receivables Account 100,000.00

1.11 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
recognize liabilities only upon delivery of goods and/or when services
are rendered and record receivables only when there is an established
valid claim against an entity or establishment.

2.10 We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the net overstatement of
the Accumulated Depreciation for the affected PPE accounts amounting to
₱1,020,514.88.
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry is as follows:
Particulars Debit Credit
Accumulated Depreciation – Buildings 516,002.39
Accumulated Depreciation - Markets 778,959.31

2,767.50

3.11 We recommended and the Management agreed to coordinate with the
DOST-CAR relative to the revised Memorandum of Agreement to
facilitate the implementation of the intended project.

3.12 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Draw the adjusting journal entry to correct the misclassification of
the fund transfer pertaining to the 20 per cent share from the fire code
fees amounting to P114,277.79 from the Due to NGAs to the
Trust Liabilities account;
b) Maintain SL for the Due to NGAs account and provide details as to
the Source Agency, PPA, and the amount received, including the
utilization/disbursements for each PPA; and
c) Exert efforts to locate documents relative to the unaccounted
balance of the fund transfers amounting to P1,234,302.75.

4.13 We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to:
a) Regularly monitor collections of income from building permits and
make a permanent and separate record thereof;
b) Reflect the breakdown of the shares of the LGU, OBO and DPWH
from the collections of building permit fees and other charges in the
Official Receipt; and
c) Ensure the timely remittance of the five per cent (5%) share of the
DPWH.

4.14 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Prepare the necessary journal entries to recognize the 15 per cent
share of the OBO from the Building Permit fees collected in the
years 2018 to 2023; and
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry is as follows:
Particulars Debit Credit
Prior Years’ Adjustments 27,687.92
Trust Liabilities 27,687.92
b) Ensure that the five per cent (5%) share of the DPWH, as well as the
fifteen per cent (15%) share from all fees and charges under PD No.
1096 are correctly taken up in the books of accounts.

5.8 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting
journal entries to correct the balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA
account and the other affected pertinent accounts for both the General
and the Trust Fund – LDRRMF.
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entries are as follows:
General Fund
Debit Credit
Cash in Bank – LCCA 5,096.00
Prior Years’ Adjustments 5,096.00
Trust Fund
Debit Credit
Cash in Bank – LCCA 11,200.00
Prior Years’ Adjustments 11,200.00

6.7 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for any noted
variances between the actual amounts in the SCBAA and SFPe.

7.9 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Maintain SLs for each road and not per project name which is
composed of several clustered roads;
ii. Allocate the cost of the road lots amounting to
₱2,193,320,099.30 to the specific road networks and provide
SLs using the MEO identified cost components; and
iii. Ensure that the SL balances reconcile with the GL balances.
b) Instruct the Municipal Treasury Office and the Inventory Committee
to coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office for the
reconciliation of discrepancies between the accounting and
property records; and
c) Exert utmost efforts to locate the Medical Equipment costing
₱60,000.00; otherwise hold the accountable officer liable.

8.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to require:
a) The Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Network Property
Card for each Road Network; and
b) The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card for each Road
Network;
ii. Ensure that the cost of projects pertaining to road networks are
taken up in the accounting records using the name of the local
road;
iii. Maintain SLs for each Municipal Road and its component
costs; and
iv. Provide adequate disclosure for the total Road Network System
in the Notes to Financial Statements.

9.13 We recommended and the management agreed to:
a) Direct the Inventory Committee to submit a copy of the RPCIs per
type of inventory to the accounting office and furnish a copy to the
Audit Team;
b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain stock
cards for each item of inventory;
c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Ledger Cards and to
compute the inventory costs using the Moving Average Cost, or the
unit cost from its latest purchase, if the moving average cost per
unit cannot be determined; and
d) Consider the hiring personnel who will take charge of the
preparation of inventory stock cards in the Municipal Treasury
Office and of ledger cards in the Municipal Accounting Office;
e) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile the RPCI and other property records with the books of
accounts and to make the necessary adjustments or corrections for
a fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.

10.6 We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to closely coordinate with each
other and regularly reconcile the cashbook balances with the accounting
records monthly or at least quarterly for timely adjustments of
discrepancies/errors that would ensure accurate and fair financial
statement presentation.

11.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Exert efforts to locate the necessary supporting documents to
establish the existence and validity for claiming the receivables;
b) Exert all efforts to collect the receivables accounts to the concerned
debtors/persons liable;
c) File the necessary request for the write-off of dormant receivables
that are deemed uncollectible, supported with completed
documentation as listed in Annex 8, COA Circular no. 2023-008,
provided with index tabs, for easy reference; and
d) Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
i. Ensure that the balances of the accounts are properly
presented in the appropriate line items of the Financial
Statements; and
ii. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the
understatement of the various Receivable accounts and the
other affected accounts.
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry is as follows:
Particulars Debit Credit
Accounts Receivable 14,098.60
Due from Officers and Employees 4,000.00
Prior Years’ Adjustments 18,098.60

12.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a) Provide in the Notes to Financial Statements disclosures on the
amount and details of the unexpended balance of the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF), in line with par
127. of IPSAS 1 and Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
and
b) Reconcile the discrepancies between the LDRRMF Utilization Report
and the accounting records.

13.8 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to:
i. Ensure that previous cash advances are liquidated and settled
in the books of accounts before a new cash advance is
granted; and
ii. Warrant that all cash advances granted for the year are fully
liquidated at year-end to ensure a fair presentation of the
Financial Statements;
b) Refrain from granting huge amounts of cash advances for personal
withdrawals by any Accountable Officer; and
c) Determine the whereabouts of the vehicle/heavy equipment
mentioned in the Sandiganbayan decision and ascertain whether
these assets were used for the benefit of the Municipality.

14.8 We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to exert more effort to locate documents pertaining to the
reciprocal accounts, reconcile and cause the elimination thereof in the
consolidated financial statements.

15.10 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that
any loss of cash or property, which is the subject of a pending request
for relief from accountability, is adequately disclosed in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

16.7 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to immediately prepare and
provide the Disposal Committee a copy of the duly accomplished and
signed IIRUP for the unserviceable properties;
b) Require the Disposal Committee to promptly evaluate the
unserviceable properties and recommend to the Local Chief
Executive the appropriate mode of disposal; and
c) Ensure that disposal of unserviceable properties conform to the
guidelines on disposal.

17.16 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Facilitate the implementation of the two (2) awarded projects and the
three (3) projects for awarding amounting to ₱850,000.00 and
₱700,000.00, respectively;
b) Ensure that problems on availability of project location,
identification of specific proponents and feasibility of the projects
are resolved first before a project is included in the AIP for funding;
and
c) Develop a well-planned procurement program to provide sufficient
time to procure the required infrastructure projects promptly within
a reasonable time.

18.11 We recommended and the management agreed to require the Municipal
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to:
a) Closely monitor the execution of the MDRRM Plan to ensure that
programs/activities are timely implemented in addressing projected
climate risks and enhancing disaster preparedness capabilities at
the LGU level; and
b) Develop a well-planned procurement program to provide sufficient
time to procure the required PPAs promptly within a reasonable
time.

19.17 We recommended and the Management agreed to require the GFPS to:
a) Create/prepare the GAD Agenda, copy furnish the Audit Team;
b) Ensure that the GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment are in
line with the GAD Agenda, supported with pertinent documents;
c) Make certain that gender issues are apparent supported by evidence
such as sex-disaggregated data, mandates are GAD-related, and
Objectives and Projects/Programs/Activities (P/P/A) are aligned with
the identified gender issues. Non-GAD-specific P/P/As are budgeted
under attributed programs subject to the use of HGDG tools to
69
determine that programs and projects undertaken are gender-
responsive.
d) Ensure that any deviation from the identified gender issues,
activities, results and/or the targets in the GPB, the reasons for the
deviations, as well as the factors that have facilitated or hindered
the implementation of the DILG-endorsed and/or LGU-approved GPB
are discussed or disclosed in the Variance/Remarks portion of the
Accomplishment Report; and
e) Inform the Local Chief Executive of any changes during the adoption
of the DILG–approved GAD Plan and Budget to the GAD
Accomplishment Report for notification of the DILG Regional Office.

19.18 We further recommended and the management agreed to judiciously
prepare the GAD Plan and Budget so that the budgeted amounts and
PPAs are implementable and realistic.

20.15 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a) Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to:
i. Prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the net
overstatement of the Due to BIR account and the other affected
accounts;
The proposed Adjusting Journal Entry is as follows:
Particulars Debit Credit
Due to BIR 19,972.71
Prior Years’ Adjustments 19,972.71
ii. Exert efforts to determine the cause of the discrepancy of the
amount remitted in January 2024 of ₱1,401,899.69 and prepare
adjusting entries if warranted to establish the correct balance
of the Due to BIR account; and
iii. Maintain SLs for each taxpayer to ensure correctness of
remitted tax withheld.
b) Ensure that all taxes withheld are remitted on time to avoid
surcharges/penalties as may be imposed by the BIR pursuant to BIR
RR No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accounting Office to: a) Restate the amount for prior periods in the comparative financial statements whenever there is a change in accounting policy that requires retrospective application; and b) Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements for the current period and each period presented, to the extent practicable, the amount of the adjustment for each financial statement line item affected due to the change in accounting policy.
observations.managementAction: The Financial Statements of the Municipality presented comparable information since uniform accounting policies were adopted for CYs 2022 and 2023.
observations.reason: No disclosures were made in the Notes to Financial Statements for PPE dropped in CY 2022, which are below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00. No PPE were dropped/reclassified due to the change in accounting policy in CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Draw adjusting journal entry to correct the misclassification of the fund transfer from PAGCOR amounting to ₱25 million; b) Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Due to NGAs account and provide details as to the Source Agency, PPA, and the amount received, including the utilization/disbursements for each PPA; and c) Exert efforts to locate documents relative to the unaccounted balance of the fund transfers amounting to ₱1,215,642.75.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has taken up the journal entries to correct the misclassification of the fund transfer from PAGCOR amounting to ₱25 million.
observations.reason:
The Municipal Accountant took up the adjustments to correct the balance of the Due to NGAs and Due to GOCCs accounts of the Trust Fund under JEV No. 3002023-06-GJEV-001 dated June 30, 2023.
No subsidiary ledgers were maintained for the Due to NGAs account.
The MACCO had not provided a breakdown of the unaccounted balance of the Due to NGAs account due to the absence of SLs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Adopt a uniform standard policy in depreciating PPEs, in compliance with the Accounting Policies provided herein and apply the same across all PPE items to come up with the correct Accumulated Depreciation for the affected accounts; and b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary journal entries to correct the net overstatement of the Accumulated Depreciation for the affected PPE accounts amounting to ₱2,819,805.54.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had adopted a uniform policy of depreciating PPEs. The Municipal Accountant took up the necessary journal entries to correct the net overstatement of the Accumulated Depreciation of the various PPE accounts under JEV No. 1002023-06-GJEV-003 dated June 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require: a) The Municipal Treasurer to prepare the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectibles for the year and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as the basis in the recognition of Real Property Tax for the year, which was in the beginning of the year pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and b) The Municipal Accounting Office to: i. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each taxpayer; and ii. Prepare the necessary journal entry to correct the net overstatement of the Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts to correct the Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the certified list of taxpayers showing the tax due and collectibles which was submitted to the Municipal Accountant to set up real property taxes receivable for CY 2023. The Municipal Accountant took up the necessary journal entry to correct the Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Special Education Tax accounts under JEV No. 100202302-GJEV-004 dated February 28, 2023.
observations.reason: No subsidiary ledgers were prepared for each taxpayer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Exert utmost efforts to locate the properties amounting to ₱4,938,674.82; and b) Facilitate the preparation of the Property Transfer Report/Deed of Donation, as applicable, with respect to the donated property amounting to ₱1,139,500.00 and submit the same to the Municipal Accounting Office. We further recommended that the management require the Municipal Accountant to: a) Prepare the necessary journal entries to derecognize the donated PPE amounting to ₱1,139,500.00 upon receipt of the complete supporting documents of the transfer; and b) Investigate the possible duplicate entries in the accounting records, with respect to the PPE amounting to ₱414,979.40, and effect the necessary adjustments, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Deeds of Donation pertaining to the donated property were submitted to the Municipal Accountant for proper recording. The Municipal Accountant took up the derecognition of the donated PPE under JEV Nos. 100202312-GJEV-PPE-102 and 100202312-GJEV-PPE-103 dated December 31, 2023. The Municipal Accountant took up the necessary adjustments of the duplicate entries under JEV No. 100202306-GJEV-02 dated June 30, 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management require: a) The Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects completed prior to CY 2019; b) The Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Network Property Card for each Road Network; and c) The Municipal Accountant to: i. Recognize at fair value road lots that were identified without corresponding cost; ii. Maintain a Local Road Network Ledger Card for each Road Network; iii. Ensure that local roads are taken up using the name of the local roads; iv. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Road Network and their components; v. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of each depreciable Local Road Network component; and vi. Provide adequate disclosure for the total Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office provided the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects completed prior to CY 2019. The Municipal Accountant took up CY 2023 road network projects using the name of the local roads. The Municipal Accountant prepared a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of each depreciable Local Road Network component.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasury Office did not maintain Local Road Network Property Cards for each Road Network. The fair value of the road lots pertaining to the local roads was taken up in the books of accounts were lumped with the cost of the Road Networks account in the General Ledger. The Municipal Accounting Office did not maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each Road Network. The Municipal Accounting Office did not maintain subsidiary ledgers for the LRN and their road components. There is no disclosure of total Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management submit to the Sanggunian a certified list of all real property tax delinquencies which remained uncollected or unpaid for at least one year indicating the reasons for non-collection coupled with a request for assistance in the enforcement of any applicable remedies of collection. We further recommended that the management intensify tax collection campaign program and enhance the collection of RPT/SET receivables, by coordinating with and/or requesting assistance from the Provincial Treasurer’s Office, to initiate or avail the following remedies: a) In addition to the issuance of demand letters, publish list of delinquent taxpayers once a week for two (2) consecutive weeks, in a newspaper of general circulation in the province, city, or municipality, pursuant to Section 254 of R.A. 7160. b) Initiate administrative/judicial action pursuant to Section 256 of RA No. 7160. The administrative remedies maybe any of the following: ▪ Seizure of personal property of the taxpayer and sale thereof at a public auction; ▪ File civil suit with the proper court; and ▪ Levy on real property and sale of delinquent property of a useable portion thereof at public auction. We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor monitor the general revision of the assessment of properties every three years as required under Section 219 of RA No. 7160, to generate additional income from increased tax assessment.
observations.managementAction: The certified list of all real property tax delinquencies was prepared and submitted to the Sanggunian. The Provincial Government of Benguet published the list of delinquent taxpayers in the Baguio Midland Courier on November 26 and December 3, 2023. The Municipal Assessor submitted an assessment of real properties in the Municipality to the Provincial Government of Benguet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Investigate the discrepancy amounting to ₱385,166.56 in the Trust Liabilities – DRRM and determine the corresponding year to which the balance pertains; b) Maintain subsidiary ledgers, by year of transfer, for the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account to ensure proper reporting thereof; and c) Ensure that the LDRRM fund yearend balances within the validity period are adequately disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The management identified the discrepancies in the Trust Liabilities – DRRM Fund and determined the corresponding year in which the balance pertains. The Municipal Accountant prepared and maintained subsidiary ledgers for the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account by year of transfer.
observations.reason: No disclosures were made in the Notes to Financial Statements for the unutilized balance of the LDRRM fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Direct the Inventory Committee to: i. Conduct a semestral physical count of supplies and materials and consider hiring or designating a Supply Officer who will take charge of the monitoring of inventory items of the Municipality; and ii. Submit a copy of the RPCIs per type of inventory to the accounting office and furnish a copy to the Audit Team; b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain stock cards for each item of inventory; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Ledger Cards and to compute the inventory costs using the Moving Average Cost, or the unit cost from its latest purchase, if the moving average cost per unit cannot be determined; and d) Require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the RPCI and other property records with the books of accounts and to make the necessary adjustments or corrections for a fair presentation of the accounts in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee conducted semestral physical count of supplies and materials, and a Supply Officer was duly designated for the monitoring of inventories of the Municipality. The RPCIs were prepared and submitted to the accounting office and the Audit team. The MTO did not maintain stock cards for each item of inventory. MACCO requested for the hiring of personnel to prepare ledger cards. The Municipal Accountant took up the adjustments of the Other Machinery and Equipment account and other pertinent accounts under JEV No. 1002023-06-GJEV-004 dated June 30, 2023.
observations.reason: The RPCIs were prepared according to source and not by type of inventory. The MTO did not maintain stock cards for each item of inventory. The MACCO requested for the hiring of a clerk for the preparation of ledger cards. There was a discrepancy in the amount of ₱801,133.34 between the total balance of the Inventory accounts as reported in the financial statements and that reported in the RPCIs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Require the Municipal Accountant to: i. Ensure that previous cash advances are liquidated and settled in the books of accounts before a new cash advance is granted; and ii. Warrant that all cash advances granted for the year are fully liquidated at year-end to ensure a fair presentation of the Financial Statements; b) Refrain from granting huge amounts of cash advances for personal withdrawals by any Accountable Officer; c) Strictly comply with the immediate liquidation of cash advances in accordance with the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002; d) Determine the whereabouts of the vehicle/heavy equipment mentioned in the Sandiganbayan decision and ascertain whether these assets were used for the benefit of the Municipality; and e) Consider requesting from Sandiganbayan copies of the disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports used for the resolution of the Malversation case.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee, in coordination with the RHU, conducted a physical count of all undistributed inventories and submitted a report thereof to the Accounting, General Services Officer and the Audit team. Copies of the disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports used for the resolution of the Malversation case were submitted to the COA on July 14, 2023. Management exerted efforts to locate the vehicle/heavy vehicle mentioned in the Sandiganbayan decision but was not able to locate the said vehicle/heavy equipment and expressed doubt as to whether the same had accrued to the benefit of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Eight cash advances amounting to ₱4.537 million were granted to the Municipal Treasurer in December 2023 for the payment of various projects and incentives of Municipal employees. Cash advances amounting to ₱5.711 million remained unliquidated at yearend. Incentives of Municipal employees are disbursed through cash advances. Cash advances are liquidated within the period prescribed under COA Circular 97-002. Management was not able to locate the said vehicle/heavy equipment mentioned in the Sandiganbayan decision and expressed doubt as to whether the same had accrued to the benefit of the Municipality. Copies of the disbursement vouchers and liquidation reports were submitted.

observations.recommendation: Prepare a request for write-off of the receivable account following the requirements and procedures cited in COA Circular 2016-005 for the fair presentation of the assets in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer’s Office to immediately prepare and provide the Disposal Committee a copy of the duly accomplished and signed IIRUP for the unserviceable properties; b) Require the Disposal Committee to promptly evaluate the unserviceable properties and recommend to the Local Chief Executive the appropriate mode of disposal; and c) Ensure that disposal of unserviceable properties conform to the guidelines on disposal.

The Disposal Committee has evaluated the IIRUP and recommended the mode of disposal of the unserviceable PPE to the LCE. Management has complied with the guidelines on disposal of PPE.
observations.reason: The IIRUP submitted by the MTO did not include the unserviceable properties amounting to ₱1.815 million. The Disposal Committee did not evaluate the identified unserviceable properties. The disposal of the identified PPE was not yet undertaken.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the management: a) Confer with the donor to clarify with respect to the condition set in the Deed of Donation and inform him of the marking requirement under COA Circular No.75-6 and the prohibitions imposed by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2010-101. If the donor consents to the removal of the prohibited markings, request that the Deed of Donation be revised to such effect. Else, decline the donation in view of the requirements and prohibitions provided by the COA and DILG Circular/Memorandum Circular; and b) Take up the donation of the vehicle in the accounting records should the donor consent to the alterations required to comply with the prescribed markings of government property.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a) The Municipal Treasury Office and the Inventory Committee include the cost of Local Road Networks in the Report on Local Road Network and the Report on Physical Count of the Road Network System, respectively;
b) The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for road projects; and
c) The Municipal Accountant: i. Recognize at fair value local roads that were identified without corresponding cost; ii. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation of each depreciable Local Road Network component; iii. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for the Road Network and their components; and iv. Provide adequate disclosure for the total Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared the Report on Physical Count of the Road Network System and indicated the cost of the Road Networks. The Municipal Accountant prepared a lapsing schedule of the depreciation of each road network.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer had not furnished the MACCO and the MTO with the complete description and cost segregation of the road components for the road projects. The lapsing schedule prepared by the Municipal Accountant was not segregated per road component. Subsidiary ledgers are not being maintained for each road network and its components. The total Road Network System was not disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to: a) Submit the missing attachments/supporting documents; b) Invite NGO, COA and private group representative at least five calendar days before the date of eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre-bid conference, bid evaluation and post-qualification; c) Conduct pre-procurement conference pursuant to Section 20 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184; We also recommended that the Municipal Engineer: a) Adopt the prescribed format of Approved Contract in DPWH D.O. 197 series 2016; b) Prepare and include PERT/CPM as part of the contract submitted to the Audit Team; and c) Prepare and include minimum requirements for a Construction Safety and Health Program for the project being considered; We further recommended that the Accountant ensure the completeness of supporting documents prior to processing of claims.

Management included the PERT/CPM as part of the contract submitted to the Audit Team. The CSHP for all projects were included in the contracts that were submitted to the Audit team. observations.recommendation:
We recommended and management agreed to comply with the following courses of action:
The incumbent OIC- Municipal Accountant and the incumbent Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe rules and regulations mandated by Section 181 of the GAAM, Volume I in the reconciliation of their respective financial records; and,
Meanwhile, the OIC-Municipal Accountant to continuously exert best effort to put the Cash in Bank –LCCA account in agreement with the cashbook and bank records to ensure reliability of the cash account adopting the procedures required in the steps of bank reconciliation.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the MCO to reconstruct and account for the difference of P7,272,093.78 to bring forth correct balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements as of December 31, 2016 and to abide by the appropriate accounting procedures required in the maintenance of the books of accounts.

We recommended management to direct the MCO to correctly maintain the SLs in such detail to provide substantial audit support for the GL and further provide breakdowns of financial statement totals.

Management agreed to comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended the OIC- Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT/SET receivables based from the Real Property Tax Account Register Taxpayer’s index cards maintained by the incumbent Municipal Treasurer with the corresponding duly certified list showing their names and the amount due and collectible for the year.

Management agreed to comply with the recommendation.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:
Identify recipients of the KKK loan, determine their whereabouts and capacity to return such proceeds, whenever possible, and if not, request for write-off following the requirements and procedures cited in COA Circular 2016-005 for the fair presentation of the assets in the financial statements.
Until such time that the request for write-off is granted, the receivables be reclassified to its appropriate accounts under the non – current assets in the financial statements.
Establish validity of the non-moving receivables totaling P250,079.95 and institute legal remedies to enforce collection thereof.

We recommended and management agreed that Municipal Officials and Employees exercise prudence in government spending.  Limit travel claims in accordance with the rates prescribed by rules and regulations and reimbursement of actual expenditures for hotel accommodations shall be allowed only if absolutely necessary and duly authorized by approving officials.

We recommended management the following:
Undertake periodic physical inventory of the PPE, prepare the RPCPPE after its completion and furnish a copy to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year in compliance to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I; and
Instruct the incumbent GSO to coordinate with the former GSO on the completion of the turn-over of accountability to facilitate the physical count of the PPE.

Management had initiated physical inventory taking of PPEs through the newly designated GSO in compliance with the audit recommendations.

To ensure that transparency and competitiveness are achieved in government procurement process, we recommend and management agreed to implement the following corrective measures:
Adopt an efficient system of procurement of goods and services to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 especially on the conduct of competitive bidding.  Resort to shopping or negotiated procurement only when the conditions provided by the law are present and subject to the prior approval of the head of the procuring entity; and
The APP should include, among others, a general recommendation for shopping as an alternative method to be employed in case of an occurrence of a contingency so that the approval of the APP by Head of the Procuring Entity would necessarily cover an approval of such recommendation; provided, that the limits indicated for contingencies are not exceeded.

We reiterated the following prior years’ recommendations that management to record the purchases of supplies and materials in the appropriate inventory account before these shall be reclassified as expense upon issuance to end-users pursuant to Section 114 of the NGAS Manual.

The OIC- Municipal Accountant stated that adjustments on the affected accounts will be undertaken instantly.
We recommended and management agreed to ensure that supporting documents are complete with relevant information so that validity/propriety of financial transactions could readily be ascertained.

We enjoined all the key players (i.e. LCE, Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Treasurer’s Office and Department Heads) in budget execution to comply with the requirements of the Budget Operations Manual, thus, budget making and budget operations will be organized and systematized.

We recommended that Management realign the 20 percent MDF allocation on Repairs and Maintenance of infrastructure projects to Capital Expenditures by way of a Supplemental Budget to conform with JMC No. 2011-1 and source this from the GF of the Municipality.

We recommended management to:
Immediately remit taxes withheld and to oblige the concerned employee to personally bear interests, penalties and surcharges that may be imposed by the BIR; and
Remit taxes withheld on or before the 10th day of the following month pursuant to BIR regulations.
